summary: The diff modifies CSVImportSection.tsx and csvParser.ts to address the file upload error. REQ-12 and REQ-13 appear substantially implemented in CSVImportSection.tsx (per-file error tracking, FileEntry.error field, error badges, upsert button enabled for good rows). However, REQ-11 is NOT implemented: csvParser.ts still uses the CDN-based dynamic import approach (Function('u','return import(u)') with sheetjs CDN URLs) rather than a bundled/npm-imported SheetJS package. The file summary confirms this, describing the approach as 'replaces the CDN script-tag injection approach with a dynamic ES module import from the SheetJS CDN .mjs build' — which is still CDN-dependent, directly contradicting REQ-11's requirement to eliminate CDN dependency.

missing_requirements.requirement: Rewrite the SheetJS loading mechanism in csvParser.ts to use a bundled or reliably imported SheetJS package (e.g., via npm import) instead of lazy-loading from a CDN, eliminating the CDN fetch failure that causes the upload error.
missing_requirements.reason: The diff shows csvParser.ts still uses CDN URLs ('https://cdn.sheetjs.com/xlsx-0.20.3/package/xlsx.mjs' and 'https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/xlsx@0.18.5/+esm') via a dynamic Function-based import. The frontend-file-summaries.txt also confirms the approach remains CDN-based. No npm package installation or static import of SheetJS is present.
